name: Red Snapper in Brodetto with Polenta
recipeInstructions:
--------------------------RED SNAPPER IN BRODETTO--------------------------
4 pounds Red Snapper, cleaned OR
4 pounds Stripped Bass, cleaned OR
4 pounds Sea Bass, cleaned OR
4 pounds Monkfish, cleaned
2 cups Vegetable Oil
1 cup All-Purpose Flour
3/4 cup Olive Oil
1 cup Onion, finely chopped
1/2 cup Scallion, finely chopped (white part only)
Salt
3 tablespoons Tomato Paste
1/4 cup Red Wine Vinegar (or more)
3 cups Hot Water
Pepper to taste
For Polenta:
============
In a heavy saucepan, combine water, butter, salt, and bay leaf.
Slowly pour in cornmeal while stirring constantly. Bring mixture to a simmer, stirring constantly, over medium heat.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Continue to stir over heat until the mixture is smooth, very thick, and pulls away from the sides of the pan, about 15 minutes.
Cover and keep warm.
For Red Snapper in Brodetto:
============================
With a cleaver, cut each fish into three pieces, leaving head and tail on.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet to about 350°F.
Dredge pieces of fish in flour, shaking off excess. Cook, turning once, until lightly golden but not cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Drain on paper towels and set aside.
In a separate large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add onion and scallion and sauté, tossing until lightly golden, about 5 minutes.
Add fish in a single layer and sprinkle with salt. Spoon tomato paste between pieces of fish and cook for about 3 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally.
Stir together vinegar and hot water and add to fish. Bring to boil, then lower the heat and simmer, uncovered, for 2 to 3 minutes.
Carefully turn pieces of fish.
Increase heat to medium-high and continue to cook until fish is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
Transfer fish and a mounded spoonful of polenta to warm serving plates.
Boil sauce vigorously until slightly thickened and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and a few drops of wine vinegar if needed.
Pour over fish and serve immediately.
